perturbation.original_text: GENERAL EXCEPTIONS. The disclosure and use restrictions imposed in this Agreement do not apply to Proprietary Material that: (a) Pinetree can show by documentary evidence was already in Pinetree's possession before the disclosure hereunder to Pinetree; (b) is hereafter disclosed to Pinetree by a third party who has no duty of confidentiality to STI in respect of it; or (c) is or becomes generally available to the public through no act or default on Pinetree's part.
perturbation.changed_text: GENERAL EXCEPTIONS. The disclosure and use restrictions imposed in this Agreement do not apply to Proprietary Material that: (a) Pinetree can show by documentary evidence was already in Pinetree's possession before the disclosure hereunder to Pinetree; (b) is hereafter disclosed to Pinetree by a third party; or (c) is or becomes generally available to the public through no act or default on Pinetree's part.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause 4(a)(b) specifies an exception only if the third party has no duty of confidentiality to STI. Removing that condition means that even if a third party breaches confidentiality and discloses to Pinetree, it would be an exception. The contradiction is then with the overall purpose of the agreement, particularly section 3 which details the obligations of Pinetree in maintaining confidentiality. This weakens the protection of STI's Proprietary Information, undermining the core intent of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: USE RESTRICTIONS. a. SOLE PURPOSE. Pinetree may use the Proprietary Information solely for the purpose of evaluating the settlement discussions, or to evaluate such information in connection with STI's performance under any settlement documents which the parties may execute. Unless <PAGE> otherwise expressly authorized to the contrary by STI in a prior signed writing, Pinetree shall not, either directly or indirectly: (a) use, apply, reveal, report, publish or otherwise disclose any of the Proprietary Information to, or for the benefit of, Pinetree or any third party; (b) use or incorporate any of the Proprietary Information in any products or services; or (c) assist, act as an agent for, or act as an advisor or consultant to, any person or entity for the purpose of developing, marketing or selling any product or service that incorporates any of the Proprietary Information. Without limiting the foregoing, Pinetree may use the names of investors provided by STI under the terms of the Settlement Documents solely for the purpose of making an internal decision about whether to convert the Convert Indebtedness then outstanding under the terms of the Convertible Promissory Note between the parties dated as of, or about, the date of this Agreement, and agrees not to contact those investors.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the exception to disclosure and use restrictions applied only if the third party disclosing to Pinetree had no duty of confidentiality to STI. The changed text removes this condition, meaning Pinetree could receive proprietary information from a third party who *does* have a duty of confidentiality to STI, and it would still be an exception to the use restrictions. This contradicts the 'USE RESTRICTIONS' section, which prohibits Pinetree from disclosing Proprietary Information to any third party, directly or indirectly. The change weakens STI's protection and creates a direct conflict with Pinetree's obligations to maintain confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: STI may take any necessary action to compel specific performance of this Agreement or to enjoin any violation of this Agreement so that Proprietary Material is not used in any manner to harm or adversely affect STI.
perturbation.changed_text: STI may take any necessary action to compel specific performance of this Agreement or to enjoin any violation of this Agreement, provided that Pinetree has been given 30 days written notice of the alleged violation and has failed to cure it within that timeframe.
perturbation.explanation: This addition introduces a requirement for a 30-day notice and cure period before STI can seek specific performance or an injunction. This directly contradicts the 'any necessary action' language, suggesting that STI could previously act immediately. This is contradictive because now Pinetree could potentially cause damage within those 30 days while having to wait for resolution
perturbation.contradicted_text: LAW; FORUM. This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Maryland, without regarding to its choice of law provisions. The parties further agree and consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Maryland; thus, in the event that either party initiates an action pursuant to or otherwise governed by this Agreement, the exclusive jurisdiction of such action shall be in Maryland. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may enforce any judgment rendered by such court in any court of competent jurisdiction. STI may take any necessary action to compel specific performance of this Agreement or to enjoin any violation of this Agreement, provided that Pinetree has been given 30 days written notice of the alleged violation and has failed to cure it within that timeframe. so that Proprietary Material is not used in any manner to harm or adversely affect STI.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed STI to take 'any necessary action' immediately. The changed text adds a requirement of a 30-day notice and cure period before STI can act. The contradicted text now includes this new restriction. This creates a direct conflict, as STI can no longer take immediate action as the original text implied, and there are now steps that they have to take, which contradicts the original assertion that they can take 'any necessary action'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement of the parties and supersedes all other agreements, negotiations and proposals, written and oral, between the parties concerning the above-described Proprietary Information. This Agreement may only be modified by a written document signed by authorized officers of both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement of the parties and supersedes all other agreements, negotiations and proposals, written and oral, between the parties concerning the above-described Proprietary Information. This Agreement may be modified by email correspondence between the parties or by a written document signed by authorized officers of both parties.
perturbation.explanation: This amendment contradicts the original clause. Originally modifications required a signed document by authorized officers. By including 'email correspondence' as acceptable modification, the original agreement requiring formal written agreement becomes uncertain and unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INTEGRATION. This Agreement contains the entire agreement of the parties and supersedes all other agreements, negotiations and proposals, written and oral, between the parties concerning the above-described Proprietary Information. This Agreement may be modified by email correspondence between the parties or by a written document signed by authorized officers of both parties. This Agreement may be executed in two (2) or more counterparts, by manual or facsimile signature, each of which shall be an original and all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement can only be modified by a written document signed by authorized officers of both parties. The changed text introduces an alternative method of modification: 'email correspondence between the parties.' This directly contradicts the original requirement for formal written agreement, making the enforceability of modifications uncertain. The contradicted text reinforces the changed text. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists, fitting the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' as a clause with conflicting implications due to placement.

perturbation.original_text: At any time upon STI's request, Pinetree shall promptly redeliver to STI, or destroy at STI's direction, all written material in any media or format whatsoever containing, reflecting or in any way derived from the Proprietary Information (including notes, summaries, copies, extracts or other reproductions, in whole or in part), regardless of whether the material was prepared by STI or on STI's behalf. The redelivery or destruction of the Proprietary Information shall be certified by Pinetree in writing to STI, and shall not in any way relieve Pinetree of its obligation of confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time upon STI's request, Pinetree shall promptly redeliver to STI, or destroy at STI's direction, all written material in any media or format whatsoever containing, reflecting or in any way derived from the Proprietary Information (including notes, summaries, copies, extracts or other reproductions, in whole or in part), provided that Pinetree retains one archival copy for legal compliance purposes, regardless of whether the material was prepared by STI or on STI's behalf. The redelivery or destruction of the Proprietary Information shall be certified by Pinetree in writing to STI, and shall not in any way relieve Pinetree of its obligation of confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: The original section 3(c) stated that Pinetree shall return or destroy all Proprietary Information at any time. Now Pinetree may retain one archival copy for legal compliance, potentially allowing them to keep and use confidential information, which contradicts the purpose of the agreement. This also creates a contradiction because the definition of Proprietary Information implies that all of that information shouldn't be retained because it is now creating a loophole to a definition and use of the information in order to keep a legal copy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DUPLICATION. Pinetree shall not copy, duplicate, replicate, decompile or reverse engineer in any manner whatsoever (whether physically, electronically, in writing or otherwise), in whole or in part, any part of the Proprietary Information without the prior written consent of STI, which consent shall be in STI's sole and absolute discretion, and will immediately notify STI in writing of any unauthorized disclosure of the Proprietary Information. At any time upon STI's request, Pinetree shall promptly redeliver to STI, or destroy at STI's direction, all written material in any media or format whatsoever containing, reflecting or in any way derived from the Proprietary Information (including notes, summaries, copies, extracts or other reproductions, in whole or in part), provided that Pinetree retains one archival copy for legal compliance purposes, regardless of whether the material was prepared by STI or on STI's behalf. The redelivery or destruction of the Proprietary Information shall be certified by Pinetree in writing to STI, and shall not in any way relieve Pinetree of its obligation of confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a provision allowing Pinetree to retain an archival copy of the Proprietary Information for legal compliance purposes, while the original text, and now the contradicted text, initially requires Pinetree to either redeliver or destroy all written material containing the Proprietary Information. This creates a direct conflict, because the exception undermines the original intent to ensure all proprietary information is returned or destroyed. This also goes against the definition of Proprietary Information. The addition of the 'archival copy' introduces a loophole and therefore, creates a strong in-text contradiction.
